name: Hot and Spicy Banana Catsup
recipeIngredient: 2-2/3 cups distilled white or cider vinegar
8 large bananas (approximately 3 pounds), ripe and fragrant
1 cup firmly packed dark brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine raisins, onion, garlic, and tomato paste in a container of food processor or electric blender. Whirl until smooth puree, adding a little vinegar if necessary to help the blending.
2. Pour the mixture into a large heavy or Dutch oven.
3. Peel bananas and cut into chunks. Puree in food processor or blender, adding vinegar if necessary to help blending.
4. Add the pureed banana mixture to the saucepan with the raisin mixture. Stir in remaining vinegar, 4 cups water, brown sugar, salt, and cayenne pepper.
5. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring frequently. Lower the heat to medium-low and cook, stirring often, for 1-1/4 hours. If the mixture thickens and threatens to stick, add enough water to keep it moist.
6. After 1-1/4 hours, stir in corn syrup, allspice, cinnamon, nutmeg, pepper, and cloves. Continue cooking, stirring frequently, for an additional 15 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Puree or strain the catsup through a fine-mesh sieve if desired. Return to saucepan and taste for heat. Add more cayenne pepper if needed (flavor will intensify after aging).
8. Wash and sanitize canning jars, lids, and bands in hot soapy water. Keep jars warm in simmering water.
9. Pour the catsup into boiling jars, leaving 1/4-inch headspace. Wipe jar rims clean with a damp cloth. Seal jars tightly.
10. Process sealed jars in a boiling water bath for 15 minutes (jars should be covered by at least 1-2 inches of water). Remove from water and cool on wire racks. Check seals. Label, date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to one year. Allow catsup to age for two weeks before serving.
